claim: Thornburg 2026 (Cell) reports JCVI-Syn3A as having 493 genes total = 452 protein-coding + 41 RNA, whereas our independent parse of CP016816.2 gave 496 total = 458 CDS + 38 RNA. The 3-gene net difference decomposes into 6 more CDS in our count (pseudogenes / untranslated features we did not filter) and 3 fewer RNA features in our count.

value.likely_explanation: Thornburg 2026 / Breuer 2019 use a curated subset of CP016816.2 CDS (excluding ~6 pseudogenes or uncharacterised features). The RNA count discrepancy of +3 is harder to explain; may be classification differences on ncRNA vs tmRNA vs misc_RNA, or a different GenBank revision.

source_detail: 452 protein-coding + 41 RNA count extracted from WebSearch snippets of the Thornburg 2026 paper via Session 11's deep-research subagent. Breuer 2019 uses 155 metabolic enzymes within the 452 CDS as the iMB155 FBA scope. Our 496 / 458 / 38 counts come from Biopython parsing of CP016816.2 in Session 3 (SHA-256 f1d1d19b3624c8391deff73ab60627478df89cd55dfafdfb67d0df14a7680fdf).
caveats: Our syn3a_gene_count fact (458 CDS / 38 RNA / 496 total) remains accurate for CP016816.2 as-parsed. This fact documents why Thornburg 2026's numbers differ, not a correction.

Layer 6 uses Breuer 2019 labels directly (455 labelled genes, from the same 452-CDS subset); our per-gene predictions map cleanly onto Breuer's set. The 6 CDS Thornburg excludes are NOT in our Layer 6 panel either - they are presumably pseudogenes that Breuer 2019 also excluded.
The +3 RNA count discrepancy (41 vs 38) is tracked but does not affect our Layer 6 work since RNA genes aren't in the Breuer-2019 essentiality labels we use.
